$comment: DURABLE roadmap spec. It holds INTENT (what each item means) and a PREDICATE (a machine check that proves whether it exists in the repo) — never a hand-written status. `npm run flowmap:roadmap` computes built/partial/unverified/missing live from these predicates, so this file cannot go stale the way a prose '**State:** ❌ Missing' line does. Editing an `intent` is a design decision; editing `checks` changes what 'done' means and must match real artifacts.
spine: understand -> (continue | design) -> plan -> review -> approve -> implement -> re-sync

keystones.1: testable understanding — makes the handover trusted (Phase B)
keystones.2: behavioural acceptance tests in the contract — makes implementation error-free, not just correctly-shaped (E2)

checkKinds.grep: { path, pattern } — regex pattern occurs in file (missing file = fail, not crash)
checkKinds.manual: { note } — declared NOT machine-verifiable yet (behavioural/visual). Never auto-passes; surfaces as 'unverified', distinct from 'missing'.

statusRule: auto = checks except manual. passed==total && !manual -> built. passed==total && manual -> partial (pieces proven, behaviour unverified). 0<passed<total -> partial. total>0 && passed==0 -> missing. total==0 && manual -> unverified.

items.title: Symbol-level completeness gate
items.intent: Every exported symbol must be a node or an explicit, listed curation exclusion. Closes the hole that caused the original drift.

items.title: Code<->map freshness in CI
items.intent: CI regenerates the node set + signatures from code and fails on divergence, not just bundle==fragments. Makes main un-stale-able.

items.title: Two-parser conformance test
items.intent: Prove the app parser (io/mermaid) and pipeline parser (mmd-parse) produce identical models, so review and enforcement never disagree.

items.title: Verification-tier metadata
items.intent: Every claim tagged verified / advisory / unverified, with a report emitting PROVEN vs NARRATED so a fresh agent can calibrate trust.

items.title: Single onboarding command (flowmap:onboard)
items.intent: One door in: proves the map green, prints the durable invariants, points at the verified artifacts, hands off the quiz. Keystone-1 entry.

items.title: Keystone 1 — comprehension self-test
items.intent: A deterministic question set generated from the verified map; a fresh agent answers from the map alone and is scored against it. Understanding becomes pass/fail.

items.title: Verified work-state (flowmap:status)
items.intent: Scenario 1. 'Where we left off' is derived per-change from the live gate (built / pending / drifted / missing), not stored prose. Kills the high-risk continuity break point.

items.title: Plan authoring + one-command dry-run cert
items.intent: Scenario 2. English -> plan.json + proposed .mmd, then one command runs apply -> stubs -> tsc -> gate and certifies the plan round-trips before a human opens it.

items.title: Authoring-time coherence
items.intent: Plan references only real ids, dependency graph acyclic, accepted set coherent — checked at authoring time, not only in the review UI.

items.title: Layout fidelity
items.intent: The review overlay must render on the human's real ctx.state positions, not a synthesised force-sim layout. The canvas is the human layer.

checks.note: Overlay must render entirely on real ctx.state positions; new nodes currently fall back to `synth`. Visual/behavioural — not grep-provable until a layout-fidelity acceptance test exists (E2).

items.title: Unified review surface
items.intent: diffWorkspace and planner overlap; collapse to one path: paste -> diff + blast radius + bodies + before/after sigs -> accept/reject -> export.

checks.note: Two overlapping surfaces (panel/diff-workspace.ts + panel/planner.ts) still exist. 'Unified' is architectural — not a grep; needs a single entry point that supersedes both.

items.title: Single approval export
items.intent: One artifact: approved .mmd + generated stubs/contracts + the gate flipped to 'unbuilt' = the build checklist.

items.title: Keystone 2 — behavioural acceptance tests in the contract
items.intent: The approved change carries acceptance criteria that generate failing tests; 'done' = signature-gate green AND those tests green. The behavioural frontier.

checks.note: No mechanism yet turns approved acceptance criteria into failing tests. This is the second keystone and the main remaining frontier.

items.title: Writeback
items.intent: Approved/implemented code updates the fragments automatically (extend scaffold --backfill/--init to add new nodes), so the loop closes without the manual fragment edit that caused the drift.

checks.note: scaffold supports --backfill/--init but does not yet add NEW nodes from an approved plan automatically; writeback is still a manual fragment edit.

items.title: CI enforces the whole loop
items.intent: A merge can't land unless the map is fresh+complete (A1/A2), the approved plan's changes are all gate-green (C1), and acceptance tests pass (E2). 'Error-free' becomes a CI property.
